annotations.label: Rate and order of convergence

anchor.snippet: the rate of convergence and order of convergence of a sequence that converges to a limit are any of several characterizations

annotations.note: Mathlib has general Landau asymptotic relations such as `Asymptotics.IsBigO`, but no verified unified definition of convergence rate or order for convergent sequences.

annotations.label: Asymptotic rates and orders

anchor.snippet: called asymptotic rates and orders of convergence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsLittleO`, `IsBigO`, `IsTheta`, and `IsEquivalent` give the general asymptotic comparison infrastructure, but not this named convergence-rate taxonomy.

annotations.label: Non-asymptotic rates and orders

anchor.snippet: called non-asymptotic rates and orders of convergence

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declaration in `Mathlib/` defining non-asymptotic convergence rates or orders.

annotations.label: Big O notation

anchor.snippet: asymptotic notation commonly called " big O notation ,"

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigO` is Mathlib's formal definition of Landau big-O notation along a filter.

annotations.label: Iterative asymptotic order and rate

anchor.snippet: For iterative methods, a sequence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigOWith` can express bounded error ratios with a constant, but Mathlib has no verified iterative order/rate definition.

annotations.label: Q-convergence

anchor.snippet: known specifically as the rates and orders of Q-convergence

annotations.note: Mathlib has quotient-limit-to-big-O infrastructure via `Asymptotics.isBigO_of_div_tendsto_nhds`, but no named Q-convergence classes were found.

annotations.label: Asymptotic error constant

anchor.snippet: may also be called the asymptotic error constant

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsEquivalent` captures leading asymptotic equality of errors, but Mathlib has no named asymptotic error constant definition.

annotations.label: Series acceleration

anchor.snippet: Series acceleration methods are techniques for improving the rate of convergence

annotations.note: Searches for series acceleration and Aitken-style terminology found no Mathlib formalization of this definition.

annotations.label: Grid discretization convergence

anchor.snippet: A sequence of approximate grid solutions

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declaration defining convergence rates for approximate grid solutions or discretization grids.

annotations.label: Metric interpretation of error

anchor.snippet: the absolute value symbols stand for a metric for the space of solutions

annotations.note: `Metric.tendsto_atTop` characterizes sequence convergence by the metric distance to the limit, but it is not a discretization-error notation definition.

annotations.label: Non-grid discretization convergence

anchor.snippet: Similar definitions also apply for non-grid discretization schemes

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declaration for non-grid discretization convergence rates or orders.

annotations.label: Asymptotically faster convergence

anchor.snippet: is said to asymptotically converge more quickly than another sequence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsLittleO` formalizes one error term being asymptotically negligible relative to another, which is the standard faster-convergence comparison.

annotations.label: Same asymptotic order

anchor.snippet: said to asymptotically converge with the same order of convergence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsTheta` formalizes mutual big-O comparison, matching the same asymptotic order relation for error functions.

annotations.label: Asymptotic equivalence

anchor.snippet: said to be asymptotically equivalent

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsEquivalent` is Mathlib's definition of asymptotic equivalence along a filter.

annotations.label: Q-order and Q-rate

anchor.snippet: The sequence is said to converge with order

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigOWith` can state the bounded quotient with a rate constant, but Mathlib has no named Q-order or Q-rate definition.

annotations.label: Need for technical rate definitions

anchor.snippet: Other more technical rate definitions are needed

annotations.note: This is explanatory prose rather than a mathematical statement I found as a Mathlib theorem.

annotations.label: Q-convergence name

anchor.snippet: This definition is technically called Q-convergence

annotations.note: I found no declaration or namespace in Mathlib defining the term Q-convergence.

annotations.label: Larger orders converge faster

anchor.snippet: Sequences with larger orders

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.isLittleO_pow_pow_atTop_of_lt` compares powers asymptotically, but Mathlib has no Q-order speed theorem for iterative sequences.

annotations.label: Smaller rates converge faster

anchor.snippet: those with smaller rates

annotations.note: `tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_lt_one` gives geometric decay for rates below one, but no theorem compares Q-rates as a named convergence class.

annotations.label: Extra decimals rate

anchor.snippet: number of extra decimals of precision per iterate

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declaration formalizing decimal-precision gain per iteration as a convergence rate.

annotations.label: Linear convergence

anchor.snippet: is called linear convergence

annotations.note: `ContractingWith.apriori_dist_iterate_fixedPoint_le` proves a geometric fixed-point error bound, but Mathlib has no named linear-convergence definition.

annotations.label: Quadratic convergence

anchor.snippet: is called quadratic convergence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigO` can express an error bound by the square of the previous error, but no named quadratic-convergence definition was found.

annotations.label: Cubic convergence

anchor.snippet: is called cubic convergence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigO` can express an error bound by a cube of the previous error, but no named cubic-convergence definition was found.

annotations.label: Secant method order

anchor.snippet: the secant method , when converging to a regular, simple root , has an order of the golden ratio

annotations.note: Searches found golden-ratio facts but no Mathlib formalization of the secant method or its convergence order.

annotations.label: Q-limit implies big O error

anchor.snippet: the convergence of the quotient implies

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.isBigO_of_div_tendsto_nhds` proves that a quotient tending to a finite value gives a big-O relation.

annotations.label: Leading order Q-error

anchor.snippet: the leading order error is exactly

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.isEquivalent_of_tendsto_one` converts a quotient limit of one into asymptotic equivalence, but the Q-error formulation itself is not named.

annotations.label: Superlinear convergence

anchor.snippet: those sequences are said to converge superlinearly

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsLittleO` can express the usual superlinear error-ratio condition, but no named superlinear-convergence definition was found.

annotations.label: Sublinear convergence

anchor.snippet: A sequence is said to converge sublinearly

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigOWith` supports explicit asymptotic error bounds, but Mathlib has no named sublinear-convergence definition.

annotations.label: Sublinear is not linear rate one

anchor.snippet: it is incorrect to say that these sublinear-order sequences converge linearly

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib theorem distinguishing sublinear convergence from linear convergence with rate one.

annotations.label: Logarithmic convergence

anchor.snippet: converges logarithmically to

annotations.note: Searches found logarithm analysis but no declaration defining logarithmic convergence as a convergence-rate class.

annotations.label: Q-convergence shortcoming

anchor.snippet: do not naturally capture the convergence behavior of sequences

annotations.note: This qualitative limitation of Q-convergence is not present as a Mathlib theorem or definition.

annotations.label: Staggered geometric progression class

anchor.snippet: One class of examples is the staggered geometric progressions

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib definition or example family for staggered geometric progressions in this convergence-rate sense.

annotations.label: Staggered progression lacks Q-linear limit

anchor.snippet: The defining Q-linear convergence limits do not exist for this sequence

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib theorem about Q-linear limits failing for staggered geometric progressions.

annotations.label: Divergent subsequential limits prevent convergence

anchor.snippet: When two subsequences of a sequence converge to different limits

annotations.note: `tendsto_nhds_unique` gives uniqueness of limits in Hausdorff spaces, which rules out convergence when subsequential limits are different.

annotations.label: R-linear convergence

anchor.snippet: is said to converge at least R-linearly

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsBigO` can state the bounding-sequence condition behind R-linear convergence, but Mathlib has no named R-linear definition.

annotations.label: Other R-convergence classes

anchor.snippet: analogous definitions hold for R-superlinear convergence

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.IsLittleO` and related relations can express analogous asymptotic bounds, but no named R-superlinear or R-order classes were found.

annotations.label: R-error bound lower bound

anchor.snippet: provides a lower bound on the rate and order of R-convergence

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib theorem about lower bounds for the rate or order of R-convergence.

annotations.label: Exact R-rate greatest lower bound

anchor.snippet: the greatest lower bound gives the exact rate and order of R-convergence

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib formalization of exact R-rates or orders via greatest lower bounds.

annotations.label: R-order and R-rate comparison

anchor.snippet: As for Q-convergence, sequences with larger orders

annotations.note: `Asymptotics.isLittleO_pow_pow_atTop_of_lt` supplies a power-comparison lemma, but not a theorem comparing R-orders and R-rates.

annotations.label: Tight R-bounding sequence

anchor.snippet: the tight bounding sequence

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib example formalizing a tight bounding sequence for R-convergence.

annotations.label: General staggered R-linear convergence

anchor.snippet: will not converge Q-linearly but will converge R-linearly

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib theorem for the Q-linear failure and R-linear success of staggered geometric progressions.

annotations.label: Geometric progression limit

anchor.snippet: The geometric progression

annotations.note: `tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_norm_lt_one` proves that powers of an element of norm less than one tend to zero, covering geometric progressions.

annotations.label: Geometric progression Q-linear rate

anchor.snippet: converges Q-linearly with a convergence rate

annotations.note: `tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_norm_lt_one` formalizes geometric convergence to zero, but Mathlib does not name the Q-linear rate classification.

annotations.label: Real geometric progression linear rate

anchor.snippet: for any initial value

annotations.note: `tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_lt_one` proves real geometric decay for ratios in `[0,1)`, but not the named linear-rate statement for all initial values.

annotations.label: Geometric series partial sums rate

anchor.snippet: sequence of partial sums of a geometric series

annotations.note: `hasSum_geometric_of_norm_lt_one` proves convergence of geometric series in normed rings, but not a named rate for their partial sums.

annotations.label: Complex geometric convergence rate

anchor.snippet: The same holds also for geometric progressions and geometric series parameterized by any complex numbers

annotations.note: `tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_norm_lt_one` covers complex powers by the norm condition, but Mathlib does not formulate the Q-rate conclusion.

annotations.label: Staggered geometric progression R-linear

anchor.snippet: The staggered geometric progression

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib example of staggered geometric progression R-linear convergence.

annotations.label: Staggered progression not Q-linear

anchor.snippet: it does not converge Q-linearly

annotations.label: General staggered geometric R-rate

anchor.snippet: Generally, for any staggered geometric progression

annotations.label: Q-superlinear sequence

anchor.snippet: converges to zero Q-superlinearly

anchor.snippet: it is quadratically convergent with a quadratic convergence rate of 1

annotations.label: Fixed point iteration

anchor.snippet: the fixed point is an attractive fixed point
